Excerpt / idea: records need to connect across the chain
Short meaning: Traceability is weak when data is separated and hard to connect.
A useful system should help people find source information quickly.

Excerpt / idea: technology should be practical to use
Short meaning: Even good tools fail if they are too hard or expensive to use.

Excerpt / idea: technology only helps when standards are shared
Short meaning: Companies need shared rules and formats to make traceability work.
Traceability becomes stronger when different stages are linked together.
The business still needs systems, training, and support behind the feature.

Excerpt / idea: people trust traceable food more when institutions are reliable
Short meaning: Trusted organisations make traceability information feel more believable.
Consumers care about who produced the food, not only the label itself.
When trust is higher, people are more willing to buy again.
